name: Flemish Brabant (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the field of the historic arms of the Duchy of Brabant
the Brabant lion (Leo Belgicus), historic emblem of the Duchy of Brabant

description: A black field charged with a yellow lion rampant with red tongue and claws (the Brabant lion), bearing on its body a red heart shield with a horizontal white bar.
history: Adopted by the Provincial Council on 17 January 1995 and approved by decree of the Flemish Government on 16 April 1996, after the 1995 split of the historic Province of Brabant. The lion is the Brabant lion of the medieval Duchy of Brabant; the red-white-red heart shield carries the livery colours of Leuven, the provincial capital.

features.symbolism: the sable field of the Brabant arms

features.role: Brabant lion (Leo Belgicus), rampant lion with red tongue and claws, bearing a red escutcheon charged with a white fess
features.symbolism: the historic lion of the Duchy of Brabant, defaced with a red and white heart shield in the livery colours of Leuven
